nativeRange.habitat: Lagoon and seaward reefs at depths of roughly 3-30 m; juveniles tend to shelter among staghorn corals in inner reef areas.

decorPreferences: live rock with caves and overhangs

diet.notes: Primarily grazes on filamentous and macroalgae in the wild. In captivity, dried marine algae (nori) offered on a clip several times per week is generally advised, supplemented with spirulina-enriched flake or pellet, and occasional meaty fare such as mysis. A consistent vegetable diet helps reduce HLLE risk.

avoidWith: conspecifics (unless in a very large system)

summary: Large Indo-Pacific surgeonfish with tall sail-like dorsal and anal fins. Generally considered reef-safe and an active grazer, but needs a long, spacious tank and is intolerant of conspecifics.
careNotes: The Desjardini Sailfin Tang is the Indian Ocean / Red Sea counterpart to the Pacific Sailfin Tang (Zebrasoma veliferum); the two are commonly confused but desjardinii is distinguished by yellow facial freckles, vertical body striping, and a blue tail with white spots. Reaches around 25-40 cm as an adult and is an active, near-constant swimmer, so a long footprint matters more than depth - tanks of at least roughly 180 cm length and ~700 L are generally advised, with some keepers recommending larger for full-grown adults. Reported tank-size guidance varies from ~500 L (ReefApp) to 680+ L (LiveAquaria, 180 US gal); the larger value has been chosen here following the tang-size caution rule. Best kept singly per tank as it is aggressive toward conspecifics and other similarly shaped tangs, though it tends to be peaceful with unrelated reef fish. Generally considered reef-safe. Like other tangs it appears prone to Head and Lateral Line Erosion (HLLE) when water quality is poor or the diet is low in marine algae/vitamins, so frequent nori feedings and stable water parameters are commonly recommended. The sharp caudal-peduncle scalpel should be respected when netting or handling. Lifespan in captivity is not well documented for this species specifically but is generally reported in the range of other large Zebrasoma in the 8-15 year window.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Care card: 180 US gal min, semi-aggressive, reef-safe, herbivore, 72-78°F, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.020-1.025, max ~16 in.

additional.name: Reef2Reef wiki
additional.notes: Designated secondary returned HTTP 403; swapped in ReefApp.net and FishBase as additional sources for cross-verification.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Confirmed 40 cm adult size, family Acanthuridae, herbivore diet, semi-aggressive toward similar species, reef safe. Reports min 500 L; the larger LiveAquaria figure (~680 L / 180 gal) was preferred per the tang-size caution rule and rounded to 700 L.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Acanthuridae / Acanthuriformes), max total length 40 cm, native range (Indian Ocean from East Africa and Red Sea east to the Andaman Sea and western Sumatra), depth 3-30 m, environmental temperature 25.4-29.1 C.
